info.title: Service Points V5
info.description:
This section describes how to retrieve delivery-address information for Postnord-locations such as Service points, Parcel-boxes, Terminals and Collect-in-store locations.Includes the Nordic region and other parts of Europe. **MyPack Collect (19)** is mainly the service that needs this information in order to route a parcel to a specific delivery location (optional service point). It will also be requirement for **Expresspaket (42)** and **Parcel (18)** when using collect-in-store/Early collect. - The additional service **"Optional Service Point" (A7)** enables the customer to choose which service point the parcel should be delivered to for collection/pickup. - The additional service **"Collect In-Store" (E4)** enables the customer to choose which of the customer’s self-provided collection points/stores the parcel should be delivered to for collection/pickup. Requires a special contract and setup with Postnord. - The additional service **"Early Collect" (F6)** enables the customer to choose service points that offers an earlier delivery of the parcel. --- **These are the main uses cases for the APIs** - Find nearest service point based on an address, postal code, city, or geo co-ordinates - Present for customers where they can collect or drop off parcels in the E-commerce platform. - What to state as delivery-party In the PostNord EDI when the additional service "optional service point" is used. - What to print as delivery-party On the PostNord Label when the additional service "optional service point" is used. _For detailed information about EDI and Labels, see our Implementation Guidelines:
https://developer.postnord.com/edi/edis-and-labels#humany-developer-portal-edi-contact=/contact/4461_ --- **Important** - The service point ID:s are not unique, you need to combine the service point ID with the country code. - Service points are changed on a regular basis, we recommend you update your local database every day. - The delivery address should be used in the EDI and on the Label - The visiting address is used to guide the customers to the service point --- **Nearest Service Points APIs** The APIs provides the nearest service points from a location defined by an address or geo co-ordinates. - Distance is based on driving route. The accuracy of what service points that are presented in the response and the distance to these service points is dependent on how detailed the information in the search criteria is. - If the search includes a full address down to street number, the service will be able to calculate an exact starting point and thus the response will be as accurate as it can be. - If the search only contains country code and city there are two different risks of getting a response that the e-buyer will not be satisfied with. - One is that if there are many cities in the country with the name in the search, the system might choose the wrong city. - The other is that the system will calculate the starting point as the center of the city. If the e-buyer lives in the outskirts of the city the presented service points might not be the ones nearest at all. The remedy for the first case is to add the postal code as a search parameter and for the second one to also add street. In some cases postal code and street are pretty much the same, hence the starting position will be almost the same regardless of what value used. --- **Service Points Information APIs** The APIs provides service points information using different types of selections; defined by the incoming query parameters. - The bypostalcode API will retrieve the default service point associated with the postal code area of notification. - The ids will get you information about a single service point - The information will get you information about the service points that meets the incoming query. These APIs can be used for multiple reasons, like informing your customers of the address and opening hours for a specific service point. --- **Service Points Delta Information APIs** The APIs provides the functionality to discover newly created, updated, or deleted service points without performing a full read of the target resource with every request. --- **API Information** | **Date** | **Version** | **Description** | | ---------- | ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | | | | | | **Feature**| **Setting** | **Description** | | ---------- | ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | | CORS | True | Enable access to the API from a different origin | | SECURED | False |The API do not require a PostNord Oauth2 login | ---

NOTE: Click on Authorization button and provide your apikey you received after signup

get.summary: Find nearest service points by coordinates
get.description:
**Description** 

Find Nearest Service Points allows you to fetch information directly to your E-commerce platform or other system.
- Query the enables applications to discover the nearest service point by coordinate. 
- The response contains an array of service points sorted by distance from the search point.

parameters.name: returnType
parameters.description: Defines the response message format

parameters.name: countryCode
parameters.description:
Defines the country where the service point is located. Country code in ISO 3166-1. 
* Allowed value is one of SE, NO, FI, DK, DE, BE, NL, LU, EE, LV, LT, AT, FR, ES, AX, PL, PT, CZ, IE, SK, SI

parameters.name: northing
parameters.description: The Northing coordinate

parameters.name: easting
parameters.description: The Easting coordinate

parameters.name: agreementCountry
parameters.description:
Used to retreive service points for the countryCode that has an agreement to be used for senders in the agreementCountry. Defined in ISO 3166-1.
* Value can be one of **(SE,NO,DK,FI)**. If countryCode is one of SE,NO,DK,FI then agreementCountry can be left empty.

parameters.name: srId
parameters.description: World Geodetic System reference (SRID), the latest revision is WGS 84 (also known as WGS 1984, EPSG 4326)

parameters.name: numberOfServicePoints
parameters.description: The number of service points to be returned

parameters.name: customerKey
parameters.description:
Used to retreive service points belong to a customer key
* **Collect In Store**

parameters.name: context
parameters.description:
A context has the logic of what types of service points to include in the response. 
* **optionalservicepoint** - active service points available for deliveries. (**Recommended value for checkout-solutions and TA-systems**)
* **earlycollect** - service points available for the addon earlycollect (**F6**)
* **saturdaydelivery** - returns service points with Saturday delivery. When paired with another context for example 'optionalservicepoint', will return all optional servicepoints with Saturday delivery
* **mypacksmall** - Used for service Mypack Small (NO). will return one service point with preference to parcel locker if within 500 meters driving distance from consumer address.
* **all** - returns all service points including service points not eligible for sorting and deliveries. (**Only use if you have a logic for filtering out the not eligible service points from reponse**)

Several contexts can be selected via a comma separated list.

parameters.name: responseFilter
parameters.description:
Defines the objects/attributes that is included in the response message to the application. 
* **public** - Standard set of objects/attributes necessary for PostNord Customer requirements (EDI, Delivery Checkouts, Map views, etc)  
* **internal** - Extended set that includes all objects/attributes defined in the swagger model (**only use if you need other data than standard set and have separate filtering and logic to manage the response**)

parameters.name: typeId
parameters.description:
Defines the type of the service point, used to retreive specified types.
* **If left empty** - all types are included
* **2** - Parcel Box Location (Nordic, Europe)
* **4** - Cancelled (DK)
* **6** - Pakkeshop med salg (DK)
* **22** - Letter Office (SE)
* **24** - Business Centre (SE)
* **25** - Servicepoint (SE)
* **37** - Servicepoint (NOR)
* **38** - Servicepoint (FIN)
* **44** - Pakkeshop (DK)
* **46** - Cancelled (DK)
* **51** - Collect in Store (Nordic)
* **54** - Delivery Office (SE)
* **61** - Servicepoint (Europe)
* **73** - Terminal pickup (SE)
* **74** - Letter terminal drop off (SE)

A comma separated list of valid typeIds

Recommended types for checkout-solutions:
* Optional service points - 4,6,24,25,37,38,44,46,54,61
 
* Parcel-boxes - 2
 
* Collect in store service points (Special agreement requred) - 51
* Early Collect (Special agreement requred) - 73

Recommended is to divide the service points, parcel-boxes and collect in store.

parameters.name: located
parameters.description:
Used to retrieve service points based on location placement
* **indoor** 
* **outdoor**
* **all**

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: JSONP enables making cross domain calls by generating script tags in the current document and expecting a result back to calls a specified callback handler.

parameters.name: whiteLabelName
parameters.description: Add a prefix called Postnord to each service point name that defines that it belongs to PostNord.

400.description: The server cannot or will not process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error (e.g., malformed request syntax, invalid request message framing, or deceptive request routing).

403.description: The server understood the request but refuses to authorize it (e.g. API-key, access-token, JWT)

404.description: The origin server did not find a current representation for the target resource or is not willing to disclose that one exists.

405.description: The method received in the request-line is known by the origin server but not supported by the target resource.

499.description: The server does not support the functionality required to fulfill the request.

503.description: The server is currently unable to handle the request due to a temporary overload or scheduled maintenance, which will likely be alleviated after some delay.

504.description: The server, while acting as a gateway or proxy, did not receive a timely response from an upstream server it needed to access in order to complete the request.

get.summary: Find the default service point by postal code
get.description:
**Description** 
- Query the enables applications to discover the default service point by postal code. 
- This is query is only valid for the countryCodes **(SE, NO, FI)**.   
- Service points Denmark are connected to more than one postal code.

parameters.name: returnType
parameters.description: Defines the response message format

parameters.name: countryCode
parameters.description:
Defines the country where the service point is located. Country code in ISO 3166-1. 
* Allowed value can be one of SE, FI, NO

parameters.name: postalCode
parameters.description:
The postal code.
* The postal code should always be presented without country code and spaces. 
* Hyphens are regarded as part of the postal code and should be included.'

parameters.name: context
parameters.description:
A context has the logic of what types of service points to include in the response. 
* **optionalservicepoint** - active service points available for deliveries. (**Recommended value for checkout-solutions and TA-systems**)
* **earlycollect** - service points available for the addon earlycollect (**F6**)
* **saturdaydelivery** - returns service points with Saturday delivery. When paired with another context for example 'optionalservicepoint', will return all optional servicepoints with Saturday delivery
* **all** - returns all service points including service points not eligible for sorting and deliveries. (**Only use if you have a logic for filtering out the not eligible service points from reponse**)

Several contexts can be selected via a comma separated list.

* **all**

parameters.description: Add a prefix called Postnord to each service point name that defines that it belongs to PostNord.

get.summary: Find the nearest service points by address
get.description:
**Description** 
- Query the enables applications to discover the nearest service point by address. 
- Always use as detailed information as possible, to get the most accurate response. 
- The response contains an array of service points sorted by distance from the search point.

parameters.name: returnType
parameters.description: Defines the response message format

parameters.name: countryCode
parameters.description:
Defines the country where the service point is located. Country code in ISO 3166-1. 
* Allowed value is one of SE, NO, FI, DK, DE, BE, NL, LU, EE, LV, LT, AT, FR, ES, AX, , PL, PT, CZ, IE, SK, SI

parameters.name: agreementCountry
parameters.description:
Used to retreive service points for the countryCode that has an agreement to be used for senders in the agreementCountry. Defined in ISO 3166-1.
* Value can be one of **(SE,NO,DK,FI)**. If countryCode is one of SE,NO,DK,FI agreementCountry can be left empty.

parameters.name: customerKey
parameters.description:
Used to retreive service points belong to a customer key
* **Collect In Store**

parameters.name: city
parameters.description:
The name of the city. 
* Mandatory, if the postalCode is not provided.

parameters.name: postalCode
parameters.description:
The postal code.
* Mandatory, if the city is not provided. 

The postal code should always be presented without country code and spaces. 
Hyphens are regarded as part of the postal code and should be included.'

parameters.name: streetName
parameters.description: Name of the street

parameters.name: streetNumber
parameters.description: Street Number

parameters.name: numberOfServicePoints
parameters.description: The number of service points to be returned

parameters.name: srId
parameters.description: World Geodetic System reference (SRID), the latest revision is WGS 84 (also known as WGS 1984, EPSG 4326)

parameters.name: context
parameters.description:
A context has the logic of what types of service points to include in the response. 
* **optionalservicepoint** - active service points available for deliveries. (**Recommended value for checkout-solutions and TA-systems**)
* **earlycollect** - service points available for the addon earlycollect (**F6**)
* **labelless** - list service points that supports labelless code drop offs earlycollect (**F6**)
* **saturdaydelivery** - returns service points with Saturday delivery. When paired with another context for example 'optionalservicepoint', will return all optional servicepoints with Saturday delivery
* **mypacksmall** - Used for service Mypack Small (NO). will return one service point with preference to parcel locker if within 500 meters driving distance from consumer address.
* **all** - returns all service points including service points not eligible for sorting and deliveries. (**Only use if you have a logic for filtering out the not eligible service points from reponse**)

Several contexts can be selected via a comma separated list.

parameters.description:
Defines the objects/attributes that is included in the response message to the application. 
* **public** - Standard set of objects/attributes necessary for PostNord Customer requirements (EDI, Delivery Checkouts, Map views, etc)  
* **internal** - Extended set that includes all objects/attributes defined in the swagger model (**only use if you need other data than standard set and have separate filtering and logic to manage the response**)

parameters.name: typeId
parameters.description:
Defines the type of the service point, used to retreive specified types.
* **If left empty** - all types are included
* **2** - Parcel Box Location (Nordic, Europe)
* **4** - Cancelled (DK)
* **6** - Pakkeshop med salg (DK)
* **22** - Letter Office (SE)
* **24** - Business Centre (SE)
* **25** - Servicepoint (SE)
* **37** - Servicepoint (NOR)
* **38** - Servicepoint (FIN)
* **44** - Pakkeshop (DK)
* **46** - Cancelled (DK)
* **54** - Delivery Office (SE)
* **61** - Servicepoint (Europe)
* **73** - Terminal pickup (SE)
* **74** - Letter terminal drop off (SE)

A comma separated list of valid typeIds 

Recommended types for checkout-solutions:
* Optional service points - 4,6,24,25,37,38,44,46,54,61
 
* Parcel-boxes - 2
 
* Collect in store service points (Special agreement requred) - 51
* Early Collect (Special agreement requred) - 73

Recommended is to divide the service points, parcel-boxes and collect in store.

get.description:
**Description** 
- Query the enables applications to discover service points using  one or more service point IDs.

parameters.name: returnType
parameters.description: Defines the response message format

parameters.name: countryCode
parameters.description:
Defines the country where the service point is located. Country code in ISO 3166-1. 
* Allowed value can be one of SE, NO, FI, DK, DE, BE, NL, LU, EE, LV, LT, AT, FR, ES, AX, PL, PT, CZ, IE, SK, SI

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Service point Id's. Several Id's can be selected via an comma separated list, i.e 10000,10001,10028. One and only one country code must be given when this parameter is supplied

parameters.name: responseFilter
parameters.description:
Defines the objects/attributes that is included in the response message to the application. 
* **public** - returns objects/attributes nessassary for PostNord Customer requirements (EDI, Delivery Checkouts, Map views, ..)  
* **internal** - returns all objects/attributes defined in the swagger model

get.summary: Find service points by parameters keys
get.description:
**Description** 
- Query the enables applications to discover service points using  defined parameter keys. This endpoint now supports gzip response. You can add **Accept-Encoding: gzip** in request header.

parameters.name: returnType
parameters.description: Defines the response message format

parameters.name: countryCode
parameters.description:
Defines the country where the service point is located. Country code in ISO 3166-1. 
* Allowed values are SE, NO, FI, DK, DE, BE, NL, LU, EE, LV, LT, AT, FR,ES, AX, PL, PT, CZ, IE, SK, SI
* If left empty the response contains all available Nordic countries service points (SE,FI,NO,DK)

Several countries can be selected via a comma separated list.

parameters.name: agreementCountry
parameters.description:
Used to retreive service points for the countryCode that has an agreement to be used for senders in the agreementCountry. Defined in ISO 3166-1.
* Value can be one of **(SE,NO,DK,FI)**. If countryCode is one of SE,NO,DK,FI agreementCountry can be left empty.

parameters.name: customerKey
parameters.description:
Used to retreive service points belong to a customer key
* **Collect In Store**

parameters.name: context
parameters.description:
A context has the logic of what types of service points to include in the response. 
* **optionalservicepoint** - active service points available for deliveries. (**Recommended value for checkout-solutions and TA-systems**)
* **earlycollect** - service points available for the addon earlycollect (**F6**)
* **saturdaydelivery** - returns service points with Saturday delivery. When paired with another context for example 'optionalservicepoint', will return all optional servicepoints with Saturday delivery
* **all** - returns all service points including service points not eligible for sorting and deliveries. (**Only use if you have a logic for filtering out the not eligible service points from reponse**)

Several contexts can be selected via a comma separated list.

parameters.description:
Defines the objects/attributes that is included in the response message to the application. 
* **public** - Standard set of objects/attributes necessary for PostNord Customer requirements (EDI, Delivery Checkouts, Map views, etc)  
* **internal** - Extended set that includes all objects/attributes defined in the swagger model (**only use if you need other data than standard set and have separate filtering and logic to manage the response**)

parameters.name: typeId
parameters.description:
Defines the type of the service point, used to retreive specified types.
* **If left empty** - all types are included
* **2** - Parcel Box Location (Nordic, Europe)
* **4** - Cancelled (DK)
* **6** - Pakkeshop med salg (DK)
* **22** - Letter Office (SE)
* **24** - Business Centre (SE)
* **25** - Servicepoint (SE)
* **37** - Servicepoint (NOR)
* **38** - Servicepoint (FIN)
* **44** - Pakkeshop (DK)
* **46** - Cancelled (DK)
* **51** - Collect in Store (Nordic)
* **54** - Delivery Office (SE)
* **61** - Servicepoint (Europe)
* **73** - Terminal pickup (SE)
* **74** - Letter terminal drop off (SE)

A comma separated list of valid typeIds

Recommended types for checkout-solutions:
* Optional service points - 4,6,24,25,37,38,44,46,54,61
 
* Parcel-boxes - 2
 
* Collect in store service points (Special agreement requred) - 51
* Early Collect (Special agreement requred) - 73

Recommended is to divide the service points, parcel-boxes and collect in store.

parameters.name: located
parameters.description:
Used to retrieve service points based on location placement
* **indoor** 
* **outdoor**
* **all**

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: JSONP enables making cross domain calls by generating script tags in the current document and expecting a result back to calls a specified callback handler.

parameters.name: fileUrlResponse
parameters.description: Generates a GZIP file containing the reponse for the request, an URL is returned associated with the stored response file. If this parameter is true then the parameters page and perPage will have no effect.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page in the paginated list. This feature is activated, when a number is given here.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: Number of results per page. Default and max is 1000.

parameters.name: whiteLabelName
parameters.description: Add a prefix called Postnord to each service point name that defines that it belongs to PostNord.

get.description:
**Description** 
- Delta query enables applications to discover newly created, updated, or deleted service points without performing a full read of the target resource with every request.

parameters.name: returnType
parameters.description: Defines the response message format

parameters.name: countryCode
parameters.description:
Defines the country where the service point is located. Country code in ISO 3166-1. 
* Allowed values are SE, NO, FI, DK, DE, BE, NL, LU, EE, LV, LT, AT, FR, ES, AX, PL, PT, CZ, IE, SK, SI
* If left empty the response contains all available Nordic countries service points (SE,FI,NO,DK)

Several countries can be selected via a comma separated list.

parameters.name: agreementCountry
parameters.description:
Used to retreive service points for the countryCode that has an agreement to be used for senders in the agreementCountry. Defined in ISO 3166-1.
* Value can be one of **(SE,NO,DK,FI)**. If countryCode is one of SE,NO,DK,FI then agreementCountry can be left empty.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description:
Defines the **startDate** of the delta timespan.
* Supports upto 30 days before the **endDate**

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description:
Defines the **endDate** of the delta timespan.
* If left empty the default value is **current date**

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Defines if the response should include the full service point information about added or updated service points.

parameters.name: customerKey
parameters.description:
Used to retreive service points belong to a customer key
* **Collect In Store**

parameters.name: context
parameters.description:
A context has the logic of what types of service points to include in the response. 
* **optionalservicepoint** - active service points available for deliveries. (**Recommended value for checkout-solutions and TA-systems**)
* **earlycollect** - service points available for the addon earlycollect (**F6**)
* **saturdaydelivery** - returns service points with Saturday delivery. When paired with another context for example 'optionalservicepoint', will return all optional servicepoints with Saturday delivery
* **all** - returns all service points including service points not eligible for sorting and deliveries. (**Only use if you have a logic for filtering out the not eligible service points from reponse**)

Several contexts can be selected via a comma separated list.

parameters.description:
Defines the objects/attributes that is included in the response message to the application. 
* **public** - returns objects/attributes nessassary for PostNord Customer requirements (EDI, Delivery Checkouts, Map views, ..)  
* **internal** - returns all objects/attributes defined in the swagger model

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: JSONP enables making cross domain calls by generating script tags in the current document and expecting a result back to calls a specified callback handler.

400.description: The server cannot or will not process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error (e.g., malformed request syntax, invalid request message framing, or deceptive request routing).

403.description: The server understood the request but refuses to authorize it (e.g. API-key, access-token, JWT)

404.description: The origin server did not find a current representation for the target resource or is not willing to disclose that one exists.

405.description: The method received in the request-line is known by the origin server but not supported by the target resource.

429.description: The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time.

499.description: The server does not support the functionality required to fulfill the request.

503.description: The server is currently unable to handle the request due to a temporary overload or scheduled maintenance, which will likely be alleviated after some delay.

504.description: The server, while acting as a gateway or proxy, did not receive a timely response from an upstream server it needed to access in order to complete the request.

get.summary: Get service point file of all Nordic countries (SE,FI,DK,NO).
get.description:
Get service point file of all Nordic countries (SE,FI,DK,NO) from S3 storage. You can download previous service point file upto 7 days from current date. This is equivalent to **/information** endpoint with parameters `countryCode:SE,FI,DK,NO` and `context:ALL` and header `Accept-Encoding:gzip`.

Download the file using the URL in reponse.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Date of file you want to download. Format should be **YYYY-MM-DD**.

403.description: This is usually returned if the file specified is non-existent. (e.g., date specified is out of 7-day range, wrong date format, etc.).

404.description: The origin server did not find a current representation for the target resource or is not willing to disclose that one exists.

405.description: The method received in the request-line is known by the origin server but not supported by the target resource.

429.description: The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time.

499.description: The server does not support the functionality required to fulfill the request.

503.description: The server is currently unable to handle the request due to a temporary overload or scheduled maintenance, which will likely be alleviated after some delay.

504.description: The server, while acting as a gateway or proxy, did not receive a timely response from an upstream server it needed to access in order to complete the request.

get.summary: Get the changes of service points of all Nordic countries (SE,FI,DK,NO).
get.description:
Get the changes of service points of all Nordic countries (SE,FI,DK,NO) from date specified upto current date. File available upto 30 days backward from current date.

Download the file using the URL in reponse.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: The date of comparison against the current date. Format should be **YYYY-MM-DD**.

403.description: This is usually returned if the file specified is non-existent. (e.g., date specified is out of 30-day range, wrong date format, etc.).

additionalDescription.example: The locker box is found on the 2:a floor

Address.description:
Visiting address is where the item is pickuped or dropped off at the service point. 
The delivery address is used in the EDI for optional service point.

label.description: The Label for the box type

id.description: The ID of the box type label

located.description: Describes if the box is located indoor or outdoor

pickupRequirement.description: Defines the requirements to be able to pickup the item

CompositeFault.description: The composite fault object containing an array of fault objects

Faults.description: Fault object with the associated code and explanation text

Coordinate.description: Map coordinates to the service point

abroadExport.description: Defines if items can be sent outside the nordic region: [true|false]

amountLimit.description: Defines the amount limit for value items

customerFacing.description: Defines the type of supported customers meeting: [B2B | B2C]

prepaidOnly.description: Defines if prepaid items can be dropped off at the service point: [true|false]

agreementOnly.description: Defines if only customers with an agreement can drop off items: [true|false]

DropOff.description: Defines the PostNord capabilities associated with/what a recipient can drop off at a service point

NotificationArea.description: Defines the postal codes which are covered by the service point

percent.description: The percentage of visits the associated day
The percentage of visits for that hour for the associated day

peakHourPercent.description: The value is based on dividing the day-hour percent with the highest day-hour percent for the service point independent on day/hour

value.description: The value for for the parameter

servicePointId.description: The unique ID used in the EDI for optional service point

name.description: Refers to the name of location

routingCode.description: Information used for labels in Finland

handlingOffice.description: Information used for labels in Finland

phoneNoToCashRegister.description: Phone number to the cash register

routeDistance.description: The distance in meter to service point from a given address

postalServices.description: Date/times when the service point handles postal services

specialDates.description: Special date/times when the service point doesn't handle postal services

popularity.description:
Defines the popularity of the location. Gives a weekly (by day and hour) how popular
that particular location is based on a count factor.

cashOnDelivery.description: Items with cash on delivery can be picked up:  [true|false]

Pickup.description: PostNord delivery capabilities e.g products, timeslots, features, heavy goods,etc

Buy.description: Defines the PostNord capabilities with regards to the customer meeting at the service point

guaranteedDropOffTime.description: A dropped off before this time will be pickup by PostNord the same day

eligibleParcelOutlet.description: Defines the rules when the service point is eligible to, select as optional service point

availableForPickupStandard.description: Standard: defines the days when PostNord delivers to the service point and the time when it is available for pickup

availableForPickupEarlyCollect.description: Early Collect: defines the days when PostNord delivers to the service point and the time when it is available for pickup

DK.description: defines the DK service points types

FI.description: defines the FI service points types

false.description: defines the NO service points types

SE.description: defines the SE service points types

compositeFault.description: The composite fault object containing an array of fault objects

fault.description: Fault object with the associated code and explanation text

paramValue_2.description: A parameter value pair is a set of two linked data items

type.description: Defines the type of URL (TRACKING, BOOKPICKUP, SERVICEPOINTFILE, etc).

apiKey.description: Provide the apikey you got after signup on portal
